html:
Software guide

Square Appointments vs GlossGenius for NYC salons.

For most NYC salons, Square Appointments is a strong fit when the business needs straightforward booking, payments, staff calendars, and broad payment tooling. GlossGenius can be stronger for beauty-focused solo operators or small teams that want a polished booking experience built around salon workflows.

By Little Fight NYC · Published May 4, 2026
Square Appointments for multi-chair teams; GlossGenius for polished solo beauty work.
What mattersSquare AppointmentsGlossGenius
Best forSmall multi-chair salonsSolo operators and small beauty teams
BookingStraightforward booking and staff calendarsPolished booking built for salons
PaymentsBroad payment toolingBuilt around salon workflows
Day to dayPractical operationsClient-facing polish

Local context

Why New York changes the decision.

NYC salons live on repeat clients, neighborhood search, reviews, staff availability, and fast booking from mobile. The booking tool needs to support that flow without burying the owner in setup work.

Square AppointmentsGlossGenius

The daily work first. The software second.

The best tool is the one that supports how customers find you, how staff capture the action, and how the work moves after that.

Decision matrix

Quick comparison.

Business typeBetter fitWhy
Solo stylistGlossGenius or SquareBoth can work; choose based on brand polish vs broader payment needs.
Small multi-chair salonSquare AppointmentsStaff calendars, services, payments, and practical operations.
Beauty solo operatorGlossGeniusPolished client-facing beauty workflow.
Wellness studio with classesMindbody or MomenceClasses and memberships need a different model.

Keep / Connect / Replace / Build

Do not buy software before you check the fit.

Keep

What works

Keep the booking link clients already recognize. Keep staff calendar habits that reduce mistakes.

Connect

What matters

Connect booking to service pages, Google profile, reminders, and follow-up. Connect review asks after completed appointments.

Replace

What drags

Replace booking pages that make services hard to compare. Replace tools that require manual confirmation for basic appointments.

Build

What fits

Build service pages that explain who each service is for. Build a follow-up flow for new clients, reviews, and rebooking.

FAQ

Fast answers.

Do salons need a full customer list?

Often not at first. Many need cleaner booking, tagging, follow-up, and visibility before a bigger customer list.

Should booking live on the homepage?

The booking path should be visible fast, but services still need enough context for new customers to choose.

Can Little Fight work with existing salon tools?

Yes. Good tools stay. We connect or clean up the path around them.

Next move

Need help choosing?

Call Little Fight if you need help choosing the platform, connecting it to your website, tracking leads, cleaning up an existing setup, or deciding whether a focused build makes more sense.
